memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with low energy and stress, making leisure tempting, but my term project is barely started and due Thursday.
I spent 30 minutes on a trivial task with minimal progress, which may have been an inefficient use of my limited afternoon time given my upcoming deadline.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial task yielded minimal progress, which is inefficient given my term project deadline and current low energy.
Spending 1 hour on a trivial task with minimal progress is inefficient, especially since my afternoon is slipping by and the project remains barely started.
Spending 30 minutes on a trivial task yielded minimal progress, which is inefficient given my term project deadline and current low energy.
Spending time on trivial tasks yields minimal progress and is inefficient given my project deadline and low energy.
I am wasting time on trivial tasks with minimal progress while my energy is low and the project deadline approaches.
Spending time on trivial tasks continues to yield minimal progress, reinforcing the inefficiency of this approach given my low energy and approaching deadline.
Spending 30 minutes on trivial tasks yields only 0.05 progress, confirming that this approach is highly inefficient given my low energy and approaching deadline.
Spending time on trivial tasks continues to yield minimal progress, confirming that this approach is highly inefficient given my low energy and approaching deadline.
Working on trivial tasks consistently yields negligible progress, confirming this approach is inefficient given my low energy and approaching deadline.
Even after 1 hour, trivial tasks only yield minimal progress (0.45), confirming this approach is inefficient given my low energy and deadline.
